checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=454,663,000 (2012-09-30) jumping to Goodwill=1,329,300,000 (2012-12-31), a ~192% quarter-over-quarter increase

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=93,415,000 (2009-12-31) vs InventoryNet=92,300,000 (2013-12-31), essentially flat while Revenues grew from 7,101,507,000 (FY2009) to 10,771,900,000 (FY2013)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: No AllowanceForDoubtfulAccounts, reserve, or specific liability-reserve line items are present in the provided series; only aggregate Liabilities=13,904,978,000 (2009-12-31) to Liabilities=21,817,200,000 (2013-12-31) is available

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-K filed 2014-03-03, followed by 10-K filed 2014-03-19, followed immediately by 10-K/A filed 2014-03-20 (next calendar day)
10-K filed 2010-02-26 followed by 10-K/A filed 2010-03-01 (3 days later)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: NetCashProvidedByUsedInFinancingActivities and NetCashProvidedByUsedInInvestingActivities series only populated through 2011-03-31 quarterly / FY2008-2010 annual, with no FY2012-2013 data provided
No allowance-for-doubtful-accounts, deferred revenue, or specific reserve concepts are included in the dataset

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Likely restatement of previously issued financial statements for FY2011-FY2013 (and possibly Q1-Q3 2013), evidenced by an NT 10-K filed 2014-03-03 (late filing notice), a 10-K filed 2014-03-19, and a 10-K/A filed the very next day 2014-03-20 that supplies materially different balance-sheet figures (e.g., Assets=23,264,300,000 and Goodwill=1,329,300,000 at 2012-12-31) than what would be expected from organic/acquisition-only growth trends seen in prior quarterly filings (Assets=19,539,105,000 and Goodwill=454,663,000 at 2012-09-30). This pattern is consistent with correction of prior misstatements in reported net income, equity, and balance-sheet accounts.

Purchase price allocation from the Dollar Thrifty acquisition (closed late 2012) drove goodwill from 454,663,000 (2012-09-30) to 1,329,300,000 (2012-12-31) and intangibles from 2,531,522,000 (2012-09-30) to 4,030,200,000 (2012-12-31); such large, rapid non-cash asset recognition creates risk of aggressive valuation assumptions and future impairment risk, particularly since combined goodwill+intangibles (5,275,500,000 at 2013-12-31) exceed total StockholdersEquity (2,858,700,000 at 2013-12-31) by ~1.85x.

Receivables grew materially faster than revenue in FY2011 (AccountsReceivableNet +19.2% vs Revenues +9.7%) prior to the Dollar Thrifty deal, suggesting possible aggressive revenue recognition or delayed collections/write-offs in the vehicle rental and licensee businesses, which combined with a persistently widening gap between net income and operating cash flow (e.g., ProfitLoss=346,200,000 vs OCF=3,589,600,000 in FY2013) could mask timing differences in fleet-related cost capitalization or receivable realizability.

top_signals: Filing irregularities: NT 10-K notices in 2009, 2013, and 2014 followed by a 10-K/A filed the very next calendar day after the 10-K (2014-03-19 -> 2014-03-20), suggestive of a rushed correction/restatement of FY2011-2013 financials

Goodwill and intangible assets ballooned disproportionately (Goodwill +192% and Intangibles +59% quarter-over-quarter at 2012-12-31) versus underlying business growth, driven by the Dollar Thrifty acquisition, creating elevated impairment/valuation risk since combined soft assets exceed total equity
Receivables grew nearly 2x faster than revenue in FY2011 (AR +19.2% vs Revenue +9.7%), before the acquisition can explain the divergence
Persistent and widening gap between net income and operating cash flow across all years, consistent with capital intensive business model but warranting scrutiny of depreciation/fleet cost accounting
Data gaps: no allowance/reserve-specific line items and incomplete investing/financing cash flow series post-2011 limit full forensic verification
